posts.text: President Trump said that some people are born with “bad genes” during an interview with Brian Kilemeade in Fox News.  The idea that genes make groups of people inherently good or inherently bad is a pseudoscience that has been used by governments in history to justify prejudice and segregation.

Secretary of defense (secretary of war) Pete Hegseth held a press conference where he talked about “no quarter“ given to Iranian combatants in the context of the Iran conflict.  No quarter is the principle by which an army does not allow enemy combatants to surrender. This is a violation of domestic and international law.

Rep. Andy Ogles, of Tennessee, published a reprehensible statement on social media against Muslim Americans.  Ogles said that they don’t belong in American society, and the Republican Party in the U.S. Congress, including speaker of the house Mike Johnson, refused to condemn it.

First Lady Melania Trump held a press conference for women’s month. The speech was not particularly well received, since it contained a string of self congratulatory statements about how successful she is.  during the speech the first lady called herself, a visionary, a humanitarian, and a philanthropist.  #melaniatrump #womensmonth

Sunny Naqvi, the US citizen who  claims to have been detained for two days by CBP, then transferred to an ICE detention facility and eventually released in a different state, has come under scrutiny after CBP released photos that show Naqvi leaving O’Hare airport on her own less than an hour after she was detained, and two sheriffs offices, say that there is no record of her detention.

Did the president need approval from Congress to attack Iran? Trump’s former Deputy National Security Advisor Nadia Schadlow says the constitution says he doesn’t. The constitution begs to differ, when it says that Congress has exclusive power to declare war.

Idaho House Bill 822 require teachers, counselors, and health care professionals to contact the parents of students who they suspect of “transitioning” because of behavior that includes changes in appearance or dress.  This represents an excessive interference in students rights and a problem for students in their family life.

What is the difference between a passport booklet and a passport card?  The department of state can issue a booklet with several pages or a simple passport card that looks like a credit card. A passport card is not valid for international air travel and many travelers got stuck at airports not knowing that. Here is the difference between a passport booklet and a passport card and why the card cannot be used for international travel  (Responding to @KATE LOVE )

Did Iran fire the missile that hit the school in Minab? People like @The Older Millennial think they have video evidence of that. But that theory has been debunked by many, including now the U.S. military.  A preliminary investigation conducted by the US military reveal that the Tomahawk was launched with incorrect coordinates

President Trump explains what information he used to decide to attack Iran. He mentioned that the information was provided by people including Jared Kushner.  Jared Kushner is not part of the Trump Administration and has no security clearance, so he would not be able to get information necessary to decide. He would also need special permission to sit in on classified briefings.  This creates questions on what information the Trump family can access regarding matters of national security.
